package main import ( "bytes" "fmt" "html" "net/smtp" "strings" ) // BuildFeedbackEmail constructs an RFC 822 compliant email string with an HTML body. // While net/smtp is primarily used for the transmission of emails, this function // prepares the message body in the format expected by smtp.SendMail. func BuildFeedbackEmail(fromAddr string, toAddr string, userName string, subject string, feedbackContent string) (string, error) { if fromAddr == "" || toAddr == "" { return "", fmt.Errorf("sender and recipient addresses are required") } // Sanitize headers to prevent header injection cleanSubject := strings.ReplaceAll(strings.ReplaceAll(subject, "\r", ""), "\n", "") // Escape user input for safe HTML rendering to prevent XSS if viewed in a browser-based mail client safeUserName := html.EscapeString(userName) safeSubject := html.EscapeString(subject) // Convert newlines in content to HTML line breaks for better readability safeFeedback := strings.ReplaceAll(html.EscapeString(feedbackContent), "\n", "
") var buf bytes.Buffer // 1. Construct Email Headers // Standard SMTP email requires headers followed by a blank line, then the body. buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("From: %s\r\n", fromAddr)) buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("To: %s\r\n", toAddr)) buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Subject: %s\r\n", cleanSubject)) buf.WriteString("MIME-Version: 1.0\r\n") buf.WriteString("Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"UTF-8\"\r\n") buf.WriteString("\r\n") // Blank line separating headers from body // 2. %s
